grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Simple past: Yes / No questions and short answers
grammarPoints.rule: Use Did + subject + the base form of the verb to make a yes/no question in the simple past. Answer with Yes, I/he/she/they did or No, I/he/she/they didn't.

chart.title: Simple Past Yes/No Questions and Short Answers

intro_examples.text: Did you wash your hands? Yes, I did.
intro_examples.jp: 手を洗いましたか？はい、洗いました。

intro_examples.text: Did you brush your teeth? No, I didn't.
intro_examples.jp: 歯を磨きましたか？いいえ、磨きませんでした。

intro_examples.text: Did he take a shower? Yes, he did.
intro_examples.jp: 彼はシャワーを浴びましたか？はい、浴びました。

table.title: Simple Past Yes/No Questions
notes: Use the base form of the verb (not the past form) after 'Did' in questions.
The auxiliary verb 'did' is the same for all subjects (I, you, he, she, we, they).
didn't is the contraction of did not.

tab1_samples.text: Did you wash your hands? Yes, I did.
tab1_samples.jp: 手を洗いましたか？はい、洗いました。

tab1_samples.text: Did you brush your teeth? No, I didn't.
tab1_samples.jp: 歯を磨きましたか？いいえ、磨きませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Did he take a shower? Yes, he did.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼はシャワーを浴びましたか？はい、浴びました。

tab1_samples.text: Did she brush her teeth? Yes, she did.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は歯を磨きましたか？はい、磨きました。

tab1_samples.text: Did she eat fruit? Yes, she did.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は果物を食べましたか？はい、食べました。

tab1_samples.text: Did he go for a walk? No, he didn't.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は散歩に行きましたか？いいえ、行きませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Did he ride his bike? Yes, he did.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は自転車に乗りましたか？はい、乗りました。

tab1_samples.text: Did she make her bed? No, she didn't.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女はベッドを整えましたか？いいえ、整えませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Did you eat a snack yesterday? Yes, I did.
tab1_samples.jp: 昨日おやつを食べましたか？はい、食べました。

tab1_samples.text: Did you wash the dishes? No, I didn't.
tab1_samples.jp: お皿を洗いましたか？いいえ、洗いませんでした。

rules.title: Always use the base form of the verb in 'Did' questions
rules.jp_title: Didを使った疑問文では常に動詞の原形を使う
rules.subtitle: Do not use the past tense form of the verb in simple past questions. The word 'Did' already shows the past.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去形の疑問文では動詞を過去形にしないでください。「Did」がすでに過去を表しています。

examples.text: Did you feed the goldfish yesterday? Yes, I did.
examples.jp: 昨日金魚にエサをあげましたか？はい、あげました。

examples.text: Did you stretch your muscles? Yes, I did.
examples.jp: 筋肉のストレッチをしましたか？はい、しました。

rules.title: Use didn't for negative short answers
rules.jp_title: 否定の短い答えには didn't を使う
rules.subtitle: didn't is the contraction of did not. It is used in negative short answers for all subjects.
rules.jp_subtitle: didn't は did not の短縮形です。すべての主語の否定の短い答えに使われます。

examples.text: Did you take the dog to the park? No, I didn't.
examples.jp: 犬を公園に連れて行きましたか？いいえ、連れて行きませんでした。

examples.text: Did they do their homework? No, they didn't.
examples.jp: 彼らは宿題をしましたか？いいえ、しませんでした。

rules.title: Simple past questions only take 'Did'
rules.jp_title: 過去の疑問文には 'Did' のみを使う
rules.subtitle: Unlike the simple present, which uses 'do' or 'does', simple past questions use 'Did' for all subjects.
rules.jp_subtitle: 現在形で do や does を使うのとは異なり、過去形の疑問文ではすべての主語に 'Did' を使います。

transforms.from: Does he take a shower yesterday?
transforms.to: Did he take a shower yesterday?

examples.text: Did she brush her teeth this morning? Yes, she did.
examples.jp: 彼女は今朝歯を磨きましたか？はい、磨きました。

examples.text: Did you play soccer yesterday? No, I didn't.
examples.jp: 昨日サッカーをしましたか？いいえ、しませんでした。

rules.title: Short answers must match the subject
rules.jp_title: 短い答えは主語と一致させる
rules.subtitle: Make sure to use the correct pronoun in the short answer that matches the question's subject.
rules.jp_subtitle: 短い答えでは、疑問文の主語と一致する正しい代名詞を必ず使ってください。

transforms.from: Did he play soccer? Yes, I did.
transforms.to: Did he play soccer? Yes, he did.

transforms.from: Did she eat fruit? No, they didn't.
transforms.to: Did she eat fruit? No, she didn't.

examples.text: Did your mom make her bed? Yes, she did.
examples.jp: お母さんはベッドを整えましたか？はい、整えました。

examples.text: Did you and Leo get exercise? Yes, we did.
examples.jp: あなたとレオは運動をしましたか？はい、しました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you ride your bike yesterday? No, I didn't.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日自転車に乗りましたか？いいえ、乗りませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you brush your teeth yesterday? Yes, I did.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日歯を磨きましたか？はい、磨きました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you play soccer yesterday? No, I didn't.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日サッカーをしましたか？いいえ、しませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you wash your hands yesterday? Yes, I did.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日手を洗いましたか？はい、洗いました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you watch TV yesterday? No, I didn't.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日テレビを見ましたか？いいえ、見ませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you do your homework yesterday? Yes, I did.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日宿題をしましたか？はい、しました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you feed the goldfish yesterday? Yes, I did.
mixed_samples.jp: 昨日金魚にエサをあげましたか？はい、あげました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you take the dog to the park? No, I didn't.
mixed_samples.jp: 犬を公園に連れて行きましたか？いいえ、連れて行きませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you have a healthy snack? Yes, I did.
mixed_samples.jp: 健康的なおやつを食べましたか？はい、食べました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you wash the dishes? No, I didn't.
mixed_samples.jp: お皿を洗いましたか？いいえ、洗いませんでした。

stem: Did she
her teeth? Yes, she did.

explanation.title: Base form of verb
explanation.body: In simple past questions starting with 'Did', the verb must remain in its base form 'brush'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女は歯を磨きましたか？はい、磨きました。

stem: Did you eat fruit? Yes, I

explanation.title: Positive short answer
explanation.body: For a yes/no question in the simple past, the positive short answer uses 'did' (Yes, I did).

tab3_quiz.jp: 果物を食べましたか？はい、食べました。

stem: Did he take a shower? No, he

explanation.title: Negative short answer
explanation.body: The negative short answer for 'he' in the simple past is 'he didn't'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼はシャワーを浴びましたか？いいえ、浴びませんでした。

stem: Did you
your bike yesterday? No, I didn't.

explanation.title: Questions with Did
explanation.body: After the auxiliary 'Did', use the base form 'ride' instead of the past form 'rode'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 昨日自転車に乗りましたか？いいえ、乗りませんでした。

stem: Did you wash the dishes? No, I

explanation.title: Negative contraction
explanation.body: The negative short answer uses the contraction 'didn't' (No, I didn't).

tab3_quiz.jp: お皿を洗いましたか？いいえ、洗いませんでした。

stem: Did they
their homework? Yes, they did.

explanation.title: Main verb base form
explanation.body: Even though 'do' is the main verb here, it must be in its base form 'do' following 'Did'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼らは宿題をしましたか？はい、しました。

stem: Did she
her bed? No, she didn't.

explanation.title: Base form of make
explanation.body: Use the base form 'make' in questions starting with 'Did'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女はベッドを整えましたか？いいえ、整えませんでした。

stem: Did you play soccer yesterday? Yes, we

explanation.title: Short answer for 'we'
explanation.body: The question is 'Did you...?' addressed to more than one person, so we answer with 'Yes, we did'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 昨日サッカーをしましたか？はい、私たちはしました。

stem: Did he go for a walk? No, he

explanation.title: Past simple negative short answer
explanation.body: For 'he' in the simple past, the negative answer is 'No, he didn't'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は散歩に行きましたか？いいえ、行きませんでした。

stem: Did you
your muscles? Yes, I did.

explanation.title: Base form of stretch
explanation.body: The question 'Did you...?' requires the base form of the verb 'stretch'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 筋肉のストレッチをしましたか？はい、しました。

stem: Which simple past question is grammatically correct?
answers: Did you wash your hands yesterday?
Did you washed your hands yesterday?
Do you washed your hands yesterday?
Does you wash your hands yesterday?

explanation.title: Did + base form rule
explanation.body: The grammatically correct past simple question is 'Did you wash your hands yesterday?' using 'Did' and the base verb 'wash'.

tab4_master.jp: 昨日手を洗いましたか？

stem: What is the correct negative short answer to: 'Did she brush her teeth?'

explanation.title: Negative short answer contraction
explanation.body: The correct short answer is 'No, she didn't.', matching the subject pronoun and using 'didn't'.

tab4_master.jp: 彼女は歯を磨きましたか？いいえ、磨きませんでした。

stem: Identify the sentence with an incorrect verb form.
answers: Did you took a shower yesterday?
Did you take a shower yesterday?

explanation.title: Incorrect past form in question
explanation.body: The sentence 'Did you took...' is incorrect because 'took' is a past tense verb. We must use the base form 'take' after 'Did'.

tab4_master.jp: 昨日シャワーを浴びましたか？（不正解の文を選択）

stem: Complete the dialogue: 'Did you and Leo get exercise?' 'Yes, we ______.'

explanation.title: Positive short answer for plural
explanation.body: 'you and Leo' becomes 'we' in the answer, which takes 'did': 'Yes, we did.'

tab4_master.jp: あなたとレオは運動をしましたか？はい、私たちはしました。

stem: What does the contraction 'didn't' stand for?

explanation.title: didn't contraction
explanation.body: 'didn't' is the standard contraction for 'did not' in English.

tab4_master.jp: 短縮形「didn't」は何の略ですか？（did not です。）

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about yesterday's bike ride.
tab4_master.jp: 昨日自転車に乗りましたか？
いいえ、彼女はしませんでした。

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about making the bed today.
tab4_master.jp: 今日ベッドを整えましたか？

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about doing homework.
tab4_master.jp: 彼らは昨日宿題をしましたか？

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about taking the dog.
tab4_master.jp: 彼は犬を公園に連れて行きましたか？

japanese.title: 単純過去形：はい・いいえ疑問文と短い答え
japanese.rule: 過去の「はい・いいえ疑問文」を作るには、Did ＋ 主語 ＋ 動詞の原形を使用します。短い答えでは、Yes, I/he/she/they did や No, I/he/she/they didn't（did notの短縮形）のように答えます。
japanese.pattern: Did + 主語 + 動詞の原形...?

examples.sentence: Did you wash your hands? Yes, I did.
Did you brush your teeth? No, I didn't.
Did he take a shower? Yes, he did.
Did she brush her teeth? Yes, she did.
Did she eat fruit? Yes, she did.
Did he go for a walk? No, he didn't.
Did he ride his bike? Yes, he did.
Did she make her bed? No, she didn't.
Did you eat a snack yesterday? Yes, I did.
Did you wash the dishes? No, I didn't.
Did you feed the goldfish yesterday? Yes, I did.
Did you stretch your muscles? Yes, I did.
Did you take the dog to the park? No, I didn't.
Did they do their homework? No, they didn't.
Did she brush her teeth this morning? Yes, she did.
Did you play soccer yesterday? No, I didn't.
Did your mom make her bed? Yes, she did.
Did you and Leo get exercise? Yes, we did.
Did you ride your bike yesterday? No, I didn't.
Did you brush your teeth yesterday? Yes, I did.
Did you wash your hands yesterday? Yes, I did.
Did you watch TV yesterday? No, I didn't.
Did you do your homework yesterday? Yes, I did.
Did you have a healthy snack? Yes, I did.

source.note: Workbook exercises mapped for past simple yes/no questions.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: too and enough
grammarPoints.rule: Use too much before noncount nouns and too many before count nouns to describe more than you want or need. Use enough before both count and noncount nouns to describe the necessary or right amount.
grammarPoints.pattern: too much + noncount / too many + count & enough + noun

intro_examples.text: I don't watch too much TV. I think I get enough exercise.
intro_examples.jp: テレビをあまり見すぎません。十分な運動をしていると思います。

intro_examples.text: I drink enough water. I don't eat too much junk food.
intro_examples.jp: 十分な水を飲みます。ジャンクフードを食べすぎません。

table.title: Amounts with too and enough
notes: Use too much with noncount nouns like soda, TV, and water.
Use too many with count nouns like chips, games, and hours.
enough means you have the necessary amount. not enough means less than you need.

tab1_samples.text: I don't watch too much TV. I think I get enough exercise.
tab1_samples.jp: テレビをあまり見すぎません。十分な運動をしていると思います。

tab1_samples.text: I drink enough water. I don't eat too much junk food.
tab1_samples.jp: 十分な水を飲みます。ジャンクフードを食べすぎません。

tab1_samples.text: Daily exercise is important. I get enough sleep every night.
tab1_samples.jp: 日々の運動は重要です。私は毎晩十分な睡眠をとっています。

tab1_samples.text: Don't eat too much sugar. It is bad for your teeth.
tab1_samples.jp: 砂糖を食べすぎてはいけません。歯に悪いです。

tab1_samples.text: Do we have enough fresh fruit in the kitchen?
tab1_samples.jp: キッチンに十分な新鮮な果物はありますか？

tab1_samples.text: Leo sometimes eats too many French fries.
tab1_samples.jp: レオは時々フライドポテトを食べすぎます。

tab1_samples.text: Don't watch too many TV shows late at night.
tab1_samples.jp: 夜遅くにテレビ番組を見すぎてはいけません。

tab1_samples.text: Is there enough water for everyone at the picnic?
tab1_samples.jp: ピクニックで全員分の十分な水はありますか？

tab1_samples.text: I don't eat enough vegetables, so I need to eat salad.
tab1_samples.jp: 野菜を十分に食べていないので、サラダを食べる必要があります。

tab1_samples.text: This room is very small. There are too many people here.
tab1_samples.jp: この部屋はとても小さいです。ここには人が多すぎます。

rules.title: Use too much with Noncount nouns
rules.jp_title: 数えられない名詞には too much を使う
rules.subtitle: Use too much before singular noncount nouns like junk food, soda, TV, or sugar to show an unhealthy excess.
rules.jp_subtitle: junk food、soda、TV、sugar などの単数・数えられない名詞の前に too much を使い、不健康な過剰を示します。

examples.jp: 炭酸飲料を飲みすぎてはいけません。

examples.text: He gets too much screen time.
examples.jp: 彼は画面を見すぎます（スクリーンタイムが多すぎます）。

rules.title: Use too many with Count nouns
rules.jp_title: 数えられる名詞には too many を使う
rules.subtitle: Use too many before plural count nouns like chips, cookies, games, or hours to show an excess.
rules.jp_subtitle: chips、cookies、games、hours などの複数・数えられる名詞の前に too many を使い、過剰を示します。

transforms.from: He plays too much video games.
transforms.to: He plays too many video games.

examples.text: I don't eat too many potato chips.
examples.jp: 私はポテトチップスを食べすぎません。

examples.text: There are too many rules in the game.
examples.jp: ゲームにはルールが多すぎます。

rules.title: Enough describes the necessary amount
rules.jp_title: enough は必要（十分）な量を表す
rules.subtitle: Use enough before both count and noncount nouns to mean the right amount. Use not enough to show a deficiency.
rules.jp_subtitle: 数えられる名詞と数えられない名詞の両方の前に enough を使い、正しい量（十分な量）を意味します。不足を示すには not enough を使います。

transforms.from: I need some more sleep. I have too much sleep.
transforms.to: I need some more sleep. I don't get enough sleep.

transforms.from: We have too many water for the run.
transforms.to: We have enough water for the run.

examples.text: I think I get enough exercise.
examples.jp: 私は十分な運動をしていると思います。

examples.text: She doesn't eat enough fresh fruit.
examples.jp: 彼女は十分な新鮮な果物を食べていません。

rules.title: Enough goes before nouns, but after adjectives
rules.jp_title: enough は名詞の前、形容詞の後に置く
rules.subtitle: When modifying a noun, enough goes before it. (When modifying an adjective, it goes after).
rules.jp_subtitle: 名詞を修飾するとき enough は名詞の前に置かれます。（形容詞を修飾するときは形容詞の後に置かれます）。

transforms.from: I am enough strong to lift it.
transforms.to: I am strong enough to lift it.

examples.text: We have enough tomatoes for the salad.
examples.jp: サラダに十分なトマトがあります。

examples.text: Don't worry, we have enough time.
examples.jp: 心配しないで、十分な時間があります。

mixed_samples.text: I drink too much soda.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は炭酸飲料を飲みすぎます。

mixed_samples.text: I get enough exercise.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は十分な運動をしています。

mixed_samples.text: I drink enough water.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は十分な水を飲んでいます。

mixed_samples.text: I don't eat too many chips.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はチップスを食べすぎません。

mixed_samples.text: I don't watch too much TV.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はテレビを見すぎません。

mixed_samples.text: I get enough sleep.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は十分な睡眠をとっています。

mixed_samples.text: He only eats one apple a week. He doesn't eat enough fruit.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼は週に1個しかリンゴを食べません。十分な果物を食べていません。

mixed_samples.text: My mom thinks we watch too many TV shows.
mixed_samples.jp: お母さんは私たちがテレビ番組を見すぎていると思っています。

mixed_samples.text: It's important to get enough sleep.
mixed_samples.jp: 十分な睡眠をとることが重要です。

mixed_samples.text: Don't eat too much junk food. It is bad for you!
mixed_samples.jp: ジャンクフードを食べすぎてはいけません。体に悪いです！

stem: I drink ten cans of soda every day. That is
soda!

explanation.title: too much with Noncount noun
explanation.body: 'Soda' is a noncount noun, and ten cans is an excessive unhealthy amount. Therefore, we use 'too much'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 毎日10缶も炭酸飲料を飲みます。それは炭酸の飲みすぎです！

stem: I watch TV for six hours a day. That is
TV shows.

explanation.title: too many with Count noun
explanation.body: 'TV shows' is a plural count noun. To describe an excess, we use 'too many'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は1日に6時間テレビを見ます。それはテレビ番組の見すぎです。

stem: She always stays up late. She doesn't get
sleep.

explanation.title: enough sleep
explanation.body: Staying up late means she is lacking the necessary amount of sleep. So she doesn't get 'enough' sleep.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女はいつも夜更かしをします。十分な睡眠をとっていません。

stem: He plays basketball every day. He gets
exercise.

explanation.title: enough exercise
explanation.body: Playing basketball daily gives him the right, healthy amount of exercise: 'enough'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は毎日バスケットボールをします。十分な運動をしています。

stem: They sleep for only five hours every night. They don't get
sleep.

explanation.title: not enough sleep
explanation.body: Five hours is not sufficient. They do not get the necessary amount: 'enough' sleep.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼らは毎晩5時間しか眠りません。十分な睡眠をとっていません。

stem: I drink fresh juice. I don't drink
soda.

explanation.title: not too much
explanation.body: 'Soda' is a noncount noun, and we want to say we avoid drinking too much of it: 'too much'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は新鮮なジュースを飲みます。炭酸飲料を飲みすぎません。

stem: It's hot today. Do we have
water for everyone?

explanation.title: enough water
explanation.body: This question asks if there is the necessary, correct amount of water for everyone: 'enough'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 今日は暑いです。全員分の十分な水はありますか？

stem: My mom thinks we watch
TV shows.

explanation.title: too many TV shows
explanation.body: Because 'TV shows' is count plural, we use 'too many' to show our mom thinks it's excessive.

tab3_quiz.jp: お母さんは私たちがテレビ番組を見すぎていると思っています。

stem: He only eats one apple a week. He doesn't eat
fruit.

explanation.title: not enough fruit
explanation.body: One apple a week is less than the healthy necessary amount. He doesn't eat 'enough' fruit.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は週に1個しかリンゴを食べません。十分な果物を食べていません。

stem: Don't eat
potato chips. They are salty.

explanation.title: too many count noun
explanation.body: 'Potato chips' is a plural count noun, so we use 'too many' to advise against an excess.

tab3_quiz.jp: ポテトチップスを食べすぎてはいけません。塩辛いです。

stem: Which sentence correctly uses too much?
answers: Don't eat too much junk food.
Don't eat too much potato chips.
Don't watch too much TV shows.

explanation.title: too much + Noncount
explanation.body: 'Junk food' is a noncount noun, making 'too much junk food' grammatically correct. 'Chips', 'shows', and 'rules' are count nouns.

tab4_master.jp: ジャンクフードを食べすぎてはいけません。

stem: Which sentence correctly uses too many?
answers: He eats too many French fries.

explanation.title: too many + Count plural
explanation.body: 'French fries' is a plural count noun, so 'too many French fries' is correct. 'Sugar', 'soda', and 'TV' are noncount.

tab4_master.jp: 彼はフライドポテトを食べすぎます。

stem: Complete: 'I don't have ______ vegetables on my plate. I need some more.'

explanation.title: not enough representation
explanation.body: Needing more indicates there is currently less than the necessary amount on the plate: 'enough'.

tab4_master.jp: お皿の上に十分な野菜がありません。もっと必要です。

stem: What is the grammatical error in: 'Do we have water enough?'
answers: enough must go before the noun water.
enough is only for count nouns.

explanation.title: Enough position with nouns
explanation.body: When modifying nouns, 'enough' must be placed directly before the noun: 'enough water'.

tab4_master.jp: 十分な水はありますか？（文法ミスの説明を選択）

stem: Identify the correct sentence.
answers: It's important to get enough exercise.
It's important to get too many exercise.
It's important to get too much exercise every second.
It's important to get enough of TV.

explanation.title: Healthy amount statement
explanation.body: 'It's important to get enough exercise.' correctly uses 'enough' before the noncount noun 'exercise' to convey a healthy habit.

tab4_master.jp: 十分な運動をすることが重要です。

tab4_master.cue: Build the advice about eating vegetables.
tab4_master.jp: 十分な野菜を食べることが重要です。
炭酸飲料を飲みすぎてはいけません。
私はチップスを食べすぎません。

tab4_master.cue: Build the sentence about getting rest.
tab4_master.jp: 十分な睡眠をとることが重要です。

tab4_master.cue: Build the statement about TV habits.
tab4_master.jp: 私はテレビを見すぎません。

japanese.title: too と enough
japanese.rule: 「多すぎる（必要以上）」を表すには、数えられない名詞の前に too much、数えられる名詞の複数形の前に too many を使います。「十分（必要な量）」を表すには、数えられる名詞と数えられない名詞の前に enough を置きます。
japanese.pattern: too much/many + 名詞 & enough + 名詞

examples.sentence: I don't watch too much TV. I think I get enough exercise.
I drink enough water. I don't eat too much junk food.
Daily exercise is important. I get enough sleep every night.
Don't eat too much sugar. It is bad for your teeth.
Do we have enough fresh fruit in the kitchen?
Leo sometimes eats too many French fries.
Don't watch too many TV shows late at night.
Is there enough water for everyone at the picnic?
I don't eat enough vegetables, so I need to eat salad.
This room is very small. There are too many people here.
Don't drink too much soda.
He gets too much screen time.
I don't eat too many potato chips.
There are too many rules in the game.
I think I get enough exercise.
She doesn't eat enough fresh fruit.
We have enough tomatoes for the salad.
Don't worry, we have enough time.
I drink too much soda.
I get enough exercise.
I drink enough water.
I don't eat too many chips.
I don't watch too much TV.
I get enough sleep.
He only eats one apple a week. He doesn't eat enough fruit.
My mom thinks we watch too many TV shows.
It's important to get enough sleep.
Don't eat too much junk food. It is bad for you!

source.note: Workbook exercises mapped for too much, too many, and enough.
